scope: ROCm HIP cooperative groups feasibility for 1xMI350 sparse-MLA/LI backward score-page producer-consumer design
read: ROCm cooperative grid launch is real on the target MI350 image: an ordinary launch reports this_grid().is_valid()==0, while hipLaunchCooperativeKernel reports is_valid()==1 and grid.sync() completes. This helps only for grid-wide synchronization and global-memory visibility. It does not create a shared LDS region across CTAs; the score page can remain on chip only when producer and consumers are inside the same workgroup or inside a lower-level persistent tile owner. A grid-synchronized producer-consumer design would still need a global-memory score page, which reintroduces the HBM round trip we are trying to remove.
mori_shmem_read: MORI shmem is symmetric GPU/global memory with P2P/RDMA put/get/signal/barrier support. It remains relevant for EP dispatch/combine overlap and remote dX return, but it is not a replacement for per-workgroup LDS/shared memory in the sparse-attention backward score-tile producer-consumer path.
decision: Keep the sparse-attention backward rewrite focused on a CK/rocWMMA/HIP tile-owner kernel: query-major MFMA score/prob/scaled-dP producer, immediate D-parallel dQ and group-aware dKV consumers, and no score/prob slab written to HBM. Do not pursue cooperative-grid global score-page handoff as the next latency candidate, and do not use MORI shmem for this on-chip score tile.
